response: 389 is a prime number. It is not divisible by any prime less than its square root (approx 19.7), including 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, and 19.
reasoning_content: We need to check if 389 is a prime number. Quick mental check: sqrt(389) is about 19.7, so test primes up to 19: 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. 389 is odd, not divisible by 3 (3+8+9=20, not multiple of 3), not ending with 0 or 5, so not 5. Check 7: 7*55=385, remainder 4, so not. 11: 11*35=385, remainder 4. 13: 13*29=377, remainder 12; 13*30=390, too high. 17: 17*22=374, remainder 15; 17*23=391, too high. 19: 19*20=380, remainder 9; 19*21=399, too high. So 389 is prime. Answer: prime.
